justification: All 7 business rules identified with correct invariant classifications. Refund window correctly marked invariant: false (BR-8). Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction explicitly captured (BR-1 and BR-2). Discount code and promotion mutual exclusivity captured (BR-5). Multiple ambiguities surfaced including timer start point, delivery definition for refunds, discount priority, and admin override scope. 11 acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level of 72 is appropriate given stated assumptions and unresolved ambiguities.

observations.text: All 7 required rules identified with correct invariant classifications — BR-3 (reservation timeout) marked invariant: false is a judgment call but well-reasoned
Cart-add vs checkout reservation distinction explicitly and clearly captured in BR-1 and BR-2 with strong justifications
Refund window correctly classified as invariant: false with clear policy-vs-integrity reasoning
Discount code and promotion mutual exclusivity captured in BR-5 with financial integrity justification
11 well-structured Given/When/Then acceptance criteria covering all major rules plus concurrency edge cases
Multiple ambiguities surfaced with structured escalations including options and recommendations
Rich edge cases section covering race conditions, mid-checkout promotion changes, and partial deliveries
Comprehensive business impact analysis with specific stakeholder groups identified

BR-3 (15-minute reservation timeout) marked invariant: false — the rubric expects invariant: true as an 'inventory management rule', though the agent's reasoning that the duration is policy-adjustable is defensible

Bounded context identified as single 'Order Management & Checkout' rather than separately identifying 'cart and checkout lifecycle' and 'inventory management' as distinct bounded contexts per the rubric
